review_text: A lovely low-key place to relax camping right on the water of the Royal Princess Bay. Not fancy but good prices, quiet and looks across the bay to the city of Albany.
Very nice and peaceful waterfront caravan park, staff very friendly, clean amenities and plenty of space.

Great friendly staff. Will be amazing when the refurbs are completed.
Fantastic views. Would be great with wi-fi.
Stayed for a week.
So much to see and do.

Great location
Caravan sites over look the bay
Great position to venture out from
A lovely site to stay right on the water. Excellent concrete pads for the motor home and great facilities supported by wonderful employees
Egrets location . Had a van camp across their site to improve their view and block mine.
Great spot had a site right on the water amenities were good, staff friendly,

We stayed on a caravan site.
Great spot, dated permanent cabins but very friendly staff and residents.
Spectacular views and ensuites clean and spacious.

Our site was on the waterfront and looking over towards Albany and the harbour was magical at night with all the lights illuminating the sky
The amenities could do with modernisation
But we’re clean. The showers were hot n strong.

Lovely site with amazing views across the water. Staff were super friendly and welcoming. Facilities and ablution blocks were clean. I would certainly recommend to stay here as it is quiet and peaceful but still close enough to Albany and other local sites.

A great CP, right on the edge of water. A short drive into Albany. The staff here are great to deal with and will work hard to accommodate extension requests. We initially only booked 4 nights and ended up staying 8.

Very accommodating  when we were delayed with car troubles. Beautiful site on the water. We will come back.

Thanks
Loved this place. We extended our stay because it was so peaceful, great value for money with large pitches, just had a chilled vibe to it 😊
Beautiful grounds and scenery. Fantastic staff.

We will definitely come back!
They are so helpful people!
The view is amazing !
Thank you again
Jade

Great view of the water and big sites. Just a bit tricky having the camp kitchen so far from most sites, it was also a bit odd driving through all the permanent sites to get to caravan sites! But overall was an easy stay and worth the view

Slice of paradise!
Location & views are superb and all the staff are super friendly & helpful.
The harbour views are magical especially early morning and sunset - often like a mirror.

We had a fantastic 3 nights of camping at Panorama Caravan Park.  Clean amenities,  camp kitchen and grounds, well located not far from cbd of Albany! Very welcoming and friendly staff!! Highly recommend staying here!!

Both times I’ve tried to stay here I’ve been messed around. They say they have room, then decide they don’t after arrival. This time I stood in reception for over 1/2 an hour while they fumbled around in their own system to figure out where my site would be, only to decide (at 4pm, so too late for me to search for & get to an alternative) that they actually had no room instead, leaving me stranded with zero interest in the ramifications in such an RV unfriendly town.
The others who came into reception during that time also had issues (one with pricing & another with power not being supplied when it was paid for), so they seem to have some serious issues. Disorganised, unprofessional, & uncaring.
Kalgan River Caravan Park saved the day last minute, & were cheaper, much nicer, & had staff that care about people.

What an incredible place. Staff are super friendly and helpful. The sites are spacious and views from anywhere in the park are magnificent. The showers and toilets are combined in an ensuite situation and are super clean and spacious. Absolutely love this place, could not recommend it more!

We loved our stay at Panorama Caravan Park. The staff were so lovely and welcoming, and we really appreciated the communication pre and during our stay having never been to Albany before. The park is only 10min out of Albany and centrally located to Torndirrup NP including The Gap and Natural Bridge and Misery Beach which were all incredibly beautiful.
Great spot to see the twinkling lights of Albany. Green grass! Teani was superb friendly and showed us our site.

Staff were amazing, so friendly & helpful(even got shown to our site) Amenities were clean, views & the size of the sites were the best we've seen. One place we'd definitely want to return to, powered site was so cheap especially during school holidays.

What can I say about this place. We where lucky enough to get a water side spot. Large area heeps of room to move (not packed in like sardines) the view was amazing it was awsome seeing pelicans swimming past and the lights at night. The toilet/shower facilities where pretty good everytime i went to use them they where spotless and we'll maintain considering some people are pigs.... it was a very relaxing park. All the staff I encountered where freindly. And Jodie was amazing didn't mind me asking questions, walked me to our site told me the local food spots.And had a awsome sense of humour. I look foward to staying again it was definitely one of the better caravan parks we have been to in a very long time.

A great little caravan park. The location is great....fabulous views across the harbour from most sites. It's out of town a bit so a bit quieter...a real bonus. Facilities are a bit older but very clean and well maintained. Sites are  large and easily accessible. The staff are fantastic....very friendly and helpful. And the pricing is much better than some of the crazy prices in Albany. If you like a quieter park where your not packed in.....you will like this.
Wonderful stay in our caravan - incredibly friendly staff and beautiful views. Facilities were well equipped - powerful warm showers, and great cooking set-up!

Spent 4 nights here during Lighting of the Sound and Panorama was by a long shot the favourite park on our travels.

Staff are all very Friendly, caring and attentive. I wish I could remember the ladys name that checked us in (22/03/26) she was wonderful and made sure we had a good site for a bigger van.

All overnight van or tent sites have the most beautiful water views looking back at Town and King George Sound.

We had the pleasure of watching The lights from our site on the Sunday night and it was one of the most relaxing evenigs we've had in a long time !

Bathrooms are spotles and well maintained.

We did not use camp kitchen but it looked quite big and tourists were happily using it.

Sites and the park itself are also well maintained and guests are given an appropriate site for any van,tent etc.

1 park on our travels did not think this was important.
Our van was to big for the site which left us feeling vunerable to someone hitting us. I could not reccomend them now and my work involves talking with tourists on a daily basis but Panorama you were perfect in every way and will be highly reccomended at every opportunity.

Booked last minute, coming from Esperance, wasn't expecting much, but this site is great and on the best side of albany to get to the better beaches. Plenty of showers never a wait, and they have heaps of pressure (awesome after the drive) for the fishos heaps of herring last light on small lures

Shorefront camping with the most perfect view of light up the sound.  The owner gave us two spots to choose from, walked in the rain to our spot and was just amazingly lovely. Showers were hot and good. Stayed one night and extremely happy I chose this caravan park.
Great communication from the staff , toilets very clean amenities were more than ample, would definitely stay again.
Another delightful stay in this picturesque park. Brilliant friendly service and views over the King George Sound to enjoy. Can’t recommend this park highly enough!
The best spot in Albany!
Friendly staff. Out of town, great view and close to the beaches.
Great camp-site right on the water which we appreciated. Toilet/shower block could do with a big revamp and refresh. Close to whale station and attractions which was great.

The first thing I noticed was in the booking stage.  The receptionist was so friendly and helpful.  They even booked us a taxi to go into town to see "Lighting the Sound".  When we arrived the helpfulness continued.  An example is:  They do not circle your spot on a site map.  They walk ahead, show you to your site and the facilities.

My daughter had lost some tent pegs, a loan set were with us ASAP.   The site location and ambiance are 5*.   Some facilities are 3*, but the friendly culture more than compensates.

All in all:  The best camp site I've EVER stayed at.

Our sites were at the front of the camp site on the grassed areas by the water. Camp sites are spaced well apart - a huge plus. Ablution facilities - en suites which makes a nice change. The camp site allows doggies. It was perfect for us, we don't have children and enjoy quiet spaces where we can spend time with friends.
The lady on the front desk was so friendly and we enjoyed a swim outside our campervan. The amenities were basic but clean. Perfect view to watch the sunrise as well!

Stunning Caravan Park, had an unpowered site on the water and it was just so incredible to wake up the sunrise over the water. I only interacted with the staff when I checked in but very helpful and nice. Highly recommend.

We loved our 3 night stay at Panorama caravan park. The views and setting are spectacular. And most importantly, the staff are so amazing, so helpful and so professional. Even my little dog poppy got a welcome pack with treats. We can’t wait to come back for longer next time and will be recommending you to everybody. Thanks again so much to the staff who made our stay as good as it could be. Robyn x
Such a beautiful spot, right on the water with amazing views. Staff were amazing and amenities were very clean.

I stayed at Panorama Caravan Park in Albany for three nights while attending the Lighting of the Sound Bicentennial event. The location was excellent, making it easy to get around the town and down to the waterfront activities.

The staff were genuinely helpful and friendly throughout my stay, which always makes a big difference when travelling. Check-in was smooth and any questions I had were handled quickly.

I travelled with my own accommodation, and the site suited that perfectly. It was convenient, well positioned, and a good base for exploring Albany during such a busy weekend.

Overall, a very positive experience thanks to the great staff and the park’s location. I’d happily stay again when visiting Albany.

Great location, relaxing site and friendly staff.
We’ll be back soon.

10/10 for customer service.  Although fully booked (so I can't comment on the venue itself), Anita returned my missed call, suggested the Albany Visitor Centre as the best source of info for bookings availability.  Enthusiastic and helpful.  I will try to book earlier next time :)

We had a wonderful 3 night stay in our camper trailer. On beautiful green grass only a few metres from the water. Huge camp sites and very peaceful. The bathroom blocks were simple but really clean with fantastic hot showers with lots of water pressure. A little out of town but it’s worth the drive. The bonus is your so close to Albanys natural wonders the Gap etc.
Love Panorama caravan park! We will definitely be back. Had a fantastic peaceful time. Location is so beautiful.
Sitting outside the caravan overlooking the bay watching the pelicans, seagulls and sunrise. It’s pretty awesome

Amazing stay at the Panorama Caravan Park. Staff were friendly and helpful. Views to die for and the peace and serenity was just what we needed. Amenities were clean and tidy. Would recommend to everyone who wants a getaway

Gorgeous stay. The staff was lovely, the grounds precious and in front of the water. We also appreciated the fact that the campsite is really close to the national park (10min drive to the start of gorgeous Bald head hike, or mystery beach!). The amenities are all in good condition and very clean.

Excellent no frills caravan park. Views are 10/10. We had a waterfront site which most sites seems to be. At the rear of the park appear to be lots of permanents. Easy drive through sites at the waterfront. Ammenities basic but clean. Shower heads weren’t amazing - could do with an upgrade there. Friendly service from the lady in the office who walked us to our site and provided our kids her own drawings to colour in, bird finding checklists she had created and gifted us a gnome to take on our travels to send photos back to the park of his adventures. It’s service like this that goes above and beyond that makes people come back. 20mins from city centre and less than 10mins to the NP. Don’t hesitate to stay.

Beryl was awesome, from our bookin, to day to day service, she was on hand, for any queries, chatting and worked long hours (my observation only) but with no complaints.  Superb location, friendly staff and onsite all timers.  Loved it.

We had the best stay at Panorama. We had our caravan and the site was huge. We stayed for 3 nights and didnt want to leave. Its not a fancy modern park  but its got character. The best part about it was Tianna. We were greeted with a friendly wave and a huge smile like we were old friends, she walked us to our spot. Nicest part was it was our son's birthday whilst we were there and Tianna went out of her way to get him a gift. Such a lovely thing to do! She truly is an asset to the park!
I live there its amazing

Loved our stay at Panorama.  It is our favorite.  Traveled with family so 2 vans which they happily put us together.  The team is just lovely and the friendliest folk you would not meet.  Fantastic views over the water to Albany town, lots of pelicans and bird life, super quiet and a lovely place to stay when visiting Albany.  Wished we could have stayed longer.  We will be booking again soon.  Thanks for a great stay!

We had a wonderful week at the Caravan park. Fantastic and very friendly staff. The office ladies were so good, and very active, doing a lot of walking.
Overlooking the harbor was so great. Hopefully the Shire of Albany will have a little common sense and allow the caravan park to keep access to these sites, they should be promoting tourism, not stymieing it.

Love  staying at the panorama cc.
Been going to there  for  years  now (when Duncan wore short pants).

Beautiful park with beautiful service. The location and views from the park are stunning. The amenities are nice and the site was in good condition. Special shoutout to the lady with the black and white glasses she was super friendly, bubbly and welcoming and we left with a smile. One of the top places we stayed in on our South West WA trip. Would recommend staying here.

We had the most beautiful stay at Panorama, the staff were so lovely and it felt like home! We had the most beautiful spot on the water front! The facilities were great, and had everything we needed for our three nights :)

Wonderful campsite great facilities and unbelievable location. Had a fabulous time. I have to add that the staff here are more than helpful, every time I called I felt really cherished as a camper there. Always friendly and accommodating. I had to return a day early to Perth unexpectedly, and they refunded me which was a delightful surprise because I really wasn’t expecting that. So all in all a really positive and happy experience! Thank you.
Needs a lot of work, amazing  view of Albany, but a lot laking.
Amenities block was clean but small/old and outdated. No paper hand towel or air dryer

We had the best view from our caravan . The girl in the office(sorry forgot her name) was so helpful and had the bubbliest personality. All the staff and helpers you have are also great. The toilets were so clean  . The huge shed was amazing and we had the best time with our friends . Thankyou so much, I hope we get a chance to go back.
Cheers Gail and Bob Stanley
Wonderful views, friendly helpful team members. We had a fantastic time  staying 3 nights and I know many people really enjoyed their time too. Highly recommended. Well deserved 5 star review.
This would have to be one of the best places we stayed at, great site for the caravan with water views galore. The staff were amazing too.

Great location and facilities.
Friendly staff and brilliant communication which made everything super smooth
Would come back again for sure!
We had a fabulous stay at site 54 at panorama. Only downfall was the pad collects water. Facilities are dated but views and service make up for that 10x. We are looking forward to returning.

A little gem on the inlet/bay, just close to Albany (10 mins drive).
Nothing fancy but right on the shore. A lovely stop.

fav location of our road trip w fantastic views and weather (unpowered site, so we were right by the water). can be very windy at night. clean toilets, many facilities and warm service. so glad we stayed two nights and happy to return!

Amazing views across King George Sound. The service was incredible...the ladies in the office are amazing. Patient, kind, enthusiastic, happy and helpful...nothing was too much trouble. A five star stay.
Lovely place to camp, right on the water, mainly full timers but still a lovely camp spot, clean and tidy.

Had a wonderful stay, only one thing I was amazed at, was the amount of dog poo scattered around the park ... even in the dog run area. According to a resident we spoke to, he said that there were a couple of permanents that just open their gates and let their dogs run free to do their business.
Other than that, it was a lovely stay at a wonderful site.
You'll deffo see us again .... we'll be back with our fur babies!!!!!

We had a great time at the panorama campsite.
We have stayed there several times now and am really impressed by the warm welcome and how the staff look after the campsite.
Over the years the facilities have always been well looked after and the addition of the new toilet block and showers is a great improvement.
Thank you again for looking after us.

Great Campsite. The staff were 10/10
And the facilities so clean, i can recommend this site!
Nice view,quite place ,clean amenities

Service for check in and pre check in correspondence is amazing. Staff were friendly and happy. Amenities were clean at all times . Showers and toilets a bit dated but overall very clean and great hot water and pressure. Location is peaceful and fab views over the sound towards the city. Park is tidy and grounds were clean from rubbish. Overall a great stay
Need to update toilets and showers and have more of them
Great spot with most site having a view over the bay. Facilities are comprehensive and toilets and showers are clean.
Amazing place to stay and amazing owners. We have been visiting for 10+ years and will always go back.
Beautiful spot on the bay. Rustic camp kitchen and laundry. Friendly staff.
Unfortunately the camp was full booked when we arrived but the staff was so amazing and nice to help us find another one!

This is a great hidden gem in Albany! The only downside is that reception / management disappear in the evenings, and so we had some silly people squeezing themselves in between sites and taking away our view. This is one of those places that would be absolutely perfect if all campers had a basic level of common decency, but unfortunately that's not the case these days.

We stayed a night in an unpowered site - we had the BEST spot and view.
Check in was kind friendly helpful - the lady advised they were full.
The park is so spread out each campsite is spread out.
Quiet.
Facilities are dated - the shower block closest to us one shower did not work - broke  shower head- but otherwise amazing spot.
Loved our stay, thank you.
Loved the location. We were very lucky to be there to see the full moon rising over the water.  Fantastic spot and staff were lovely. Motorhome  holiday.

Thanks to the bubbly receptionist who welcomed us with so much kindness, what a warm welcome! The campground was fully booked, but she didn’t give up on us and managed to find us a beautiful spot. A lovely encounter. The campground itself is also great, despite the common areas being a bit dated. Highly recommended 👍🏻
Zoé & Thibault

The staff is really friendly and super helpful. Some areas of the site are a bit rundown, but the common facilities work and are clean.
From the campsite the view is stunning and you can watch the local crab fishers at night.
Beautiful spot, friendly staff, the location is relaxing,
Thoroughly enjoyed our stay at Panorama CP. No fuss park in a peaceful setting.

Great Caravan Park. Fantastic staff. Good size van sites, level. Amenities dated but work great. Dump point. Can have fire in a pit. 15 minute drive to town and close to Wind farm and Whaling Station,The Gap and some awesome beaches, walks etc.

Super delighted with our stay at Panorama! The staff helped in squeezing us in for an unpowered site right by the water at late notice. The staff were super lovely and accommodating and the atmosphere here is so peaceful. It was fantastic to look out onto the water over sunset and then be treated to a moonrise over the bay. The area is very quiet and has lots of lovely birds, pelicans etc around which add to the ambience. The bathrooms were always clean and had nice hot water showers. I never ran into anyone else in the bathrooms while I was there, so I felt I had them all to myself. Overall a super lovely, peaceful stay and we’d definitely be back.
Another great stay at panarama. It is an amazing caravan park, the view is incredible. Thanks to everyone at the park, special thanks Dunc, Aneeta, Jasmin, Charlie, Ronnie. See you all soon x
Perfect place for family gethering
Great park, sites close to the bay. Amenities kept very clean.
Had a wonderful stay here, easy to check in after hours too. Great amenities and beautiful scenery!

This was our first time in Albany and we absolutely loved this campsite! Such a beautiful location, everyone is so friendly. Amenities are clean and easily accessible and the location is great to go to both the beach and the city centre.  We camped in a tent on an unpowered site for 4 nights. We could fully relax here for our holiday and will definitely be coming back when we visit Albany again 🩷

Je voulais tellement venir dans ce camping en y lisant les avis et effectivement le cadre est magnifique. Le lever de soleil et les lumières de la ville sont sympas. Et l’accueil par Jasmine est chaleureux !!

CEPENDANT cela ne fait pas tout. La cuisine est sale et ll n’y a que 4 douches au total pour tous les campeurs !!! j’ai trouvé les toilettes sales tout le temps  et ça me dégoûtait un peu d’y accéder car il n’y a qu’un passage par jour de 10 à 11h mais beaucoup de passages de campeurs. Le sol est donc en permanence mouillé avec des traces de chaussures et les gens n’y font pas attention, de plus effectivement il y a une partie résidents et lors de notre séjour 3 enfants sont venus jouer au milieu des campeurs, ils étaient vraiment bruyants et peu respectueux des autres. Ils faisaient des doigts d’honneur. Il y a avait deux jeunes garçon et une jeune fille qui a grimpé sur l’un des arbres à au moins 5 ou 6 mètres de hauteur en arrachant les feuilles pour les jeter sur ses camarades, ainsi que des branches entières !!!!! ce sont de si jolis arbres qui font tout le charme de l’endroit. La petite a failli mourir car elle est tombée ET heureusement qu’elle a pu se rattraper sur l’avant dernière branche de justesse car je pense qu’elle en serait morte autrement. Ils ont ramassé toutes les branches avant de les jeter dans la mer et de partir en trombe avec leurs vélos.

Ce n’est pas contre le camping mais j’espère que les familles qui habitent aux alentours pourront prendre connaissance de cela pour éviter un drame familial si cela devait se reproduire.

Au vu des nuisances également d’autres campeurs et de cela je suis contente d’en partir et je trouve ça vraiment dommage car l’endroit quand il est respecté et propre est un havre de paix.
Excellent caravan park! Incredible views and location. Staff were incredibly kind and accommodating. We will definitely be back.
Amazing views, great central location, clean facilities that were all in great working order. Would definitely come back!

Ogenschijnlijk lijkt dit park mooi. Wij hadden alleen wel echt de mooiste plek op het park, de camperspots aan het water. Echter als je de andere kant op keek zag je (vervallen) caravans en huisjes die bezet worden door permanent residents. Dit maakt de sfeer van de camping wel anders. De charme is er ook wel van af. Veel honden. De faciliteiten waren verouderd. Voor ons was het prima voor een paar nachten want wij hadden een campingspot met elektriciteit en water dus konden zelf in de camper douchen/ naar toilet. Maar als je niet zelfvoorzienend bent, kan dit een nadeel zijn. Het park zit wel erg dichtbij Torndirrup NP, wat voor ons perfect was.
Spacious powered sites right by the waters edge, very friendly and accommodating staff
Lovely place we camped at on the last night of our WA roadtrip. Right on the water. Hot showers, kitchen, has everything you’ll need! Very friendly as well
Fantastic caravan park, very nice open spots especially on the water front. Clean amenities & friendly staff.
It's an older but clean caravan park and the views are amazing.  Staff were very friendly and nothing too much trouble.  Would definitely stay there again

This caravan park has a nice location and friendly staff however upon seeing other reviews, we are a little disappointed we got no map or info sheet when we checked in because we couldnt find the laundry or kitchen so assumed there wasnt any. The park is predominantly filled with long term residents. There were men ( residents) standing outside the female bathrooms rating women and girls about how they looked etc and making comments about sexual things towards anyone who went to the bathroom. There were 3 showers for everyone including the long term people ( there may have been another bathroom on the other side somewhere we didnt find one) so it was hard to actually get a shower because theres not really enough for everyone.  The park is pet friendly however we would not recommend this park for pet owners. A residents loose agressive large dog attempted to attack our dog at 6 am whilst the owner stood there and watched. We were woken up by this and the owner did not grab his dog he just stood there and watched. This dog was very agressive and out of control, our dog was tied up and sleeping until he was awoken to this dog ontop of him. It is park rules for everyone to have a dog on a leash but not one resident had their dog on a leash in the park they were just loose when out walking. Hopefully management can speak to their residents to prevent dog attacks because this could have been so much worse if we didnt get up and intervene. Despite this, the park is reasonably priced and a nice place for a short stay.

The location of this park is amazing...the staff are awesome...soo helpful & accommodating...
there are upgrades being done which it is in need of...only negative is it does need more showers & toilets for guests...it gets very busy in the mornings and people are having to line up...otherwise keep up the great work..🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

It's our second time in the same spot at Panorama Caravan Park. There are showers, toilets, a kitchen, a laundry, and a dump point. There are options for tents, caravans, campervans & motorhomes (unpowered and powered). This place is pet and kid-friendly. The staff is helpful and polite. The facilities are a little old, but clean/ well-maintained. We caught two fish in there (there is a fish cleansing station). The view is amazing.
What a orsom place to park up, exalent customer service, wharf and city views
Great camp-site opposite the town of Albany the lights of a night across the water is magic
Can't complain for the view, so fantastic

Another stay at a fabulous family run Van park. Panorama Van park is the only place we stay when visiting Albany. Fantastic staff and always willing to help. The grounds are always looked after and kept clean and so are the facilities.

Glenn(Bluey)L

Staff are not friendly. They charged me a full day for only sleeping over the night for hours.. One lady called 'T...A' was very intimdating and disrespectful.  Place was not good. Dogs dirt everywhete, water side is dirty with a lot of mosquitose. Some People there were not friendly. Dogs everywhere. I will never go back again. It was a very unhealthy environment.

This park is great as it’s a little out of town so more quiet and peaceful. It’s an older park but the facilities are extremely clean - we were even able to have a fire which was great. The young lady who checked us in gave us so much local information and tips that we didn’t even need to go the information centre - highly recommend

Great location right by the Albany bay. Amenities are old but very clean
There was a bit of noise at night from the Albany port but not too bad
Great location with great views

We stayed in an un powered site right on the waters edge, wow so amazing what a place. One of the best sunrises we have seen, stunning views. Has toilet and shower they are old , need some work but do the job. Has a camp kitchen,  laundry. Has alot of tourist places near by. The view and site was the best part, office staff very friendly 👌.
What a fabulous find. Great location, quiet and large sites. The staff were very kind and helpful. Never before have we been shown to our site personally.

Beautiful sites by the water, decent spaced camp kitchen. We booked a powered site for 4 days but a storm front came through after our first night and we needed to change to a hotel as our swag couldn't handle the weather. The staff were lovely and even refunded us the remaining days which was unexpectedly kind. Would definitely stay here again in good weather or if we had a caravan etc.

I stayed at Panorama for my first night in Perth. I booked a powered site and the one they gave me was beautiful. Great location, lovely view and easy to navigate. I was only there for one night and arrived late/left early so wasn't able to spend much time around the park. Staff were friendly and very accommodating for a late check in.
Only negative would be the bathroom facilities were a bit messy. Water and dirt over the floors and not much storage space to put your belongings down while you shower.

Staying here was an absolute delight. Friendly and helpful staff and walked with us to show us our site.

Loved the morning sunrise over the water and our dog had a great time there too.

Yes, the amenities are older but they were always very clean and no maintenance issues.

Convenient location and half way between town and the national park.
An amazing place to relax and chill with friends or family. We requested a place near water and it was worth it. Loved the view.

Had an amazing time at Panorama caravan park. The staff was incredibly helpful and friendly, and there was such a great cooperative vibe among the campers. Everyone worked together to create a relaxed, community-driven atmosphere—definitely recommend for anyone looking for a peaceful getaway in nature.
Nice campsite easily accessible to main town but in a more quiet area. Good facilities and very friendly staff
Right on the beach, awesome views, staff willing to go above and beyond to help out... I love that it's not over the top, it's in its naturalist form.

** Edit in response to the owner comment**

Umm you DID kick out over half a dozen long term residents this year.  Call it what you like but you DID kick us out. It may have been legal but not moral. I was there for over a decade and as written in my eviction notice - a good tenant.
What gets me more than anything is the hypocrisy - you stated in the news ‘we don’t want to see anyone left without place to go, if we can help by providing some secure options we believe it’s our responsibility’ WELL - how does that fit with those of us you have kicked out? We all had no where to go and were never offered options to move elsewhere in the park as you wanted the front rows for overnighter$. And here you are wanting to bring more permanent tenants in… how in the world is this fair or respectful.

What about the half dozen people Panorama Caravan park recently kicked out??? People in their 70’s, people with disabilities. They had no-where to go? Wow, what BS

Beautiful waterfront campsite overseeing amazing sunsets and sunrises everyday!!

It’s only 10 minutes away from the Torndirrup National Park!

Very friendly staff! Thank you Jasmine for going above and beyond to ensure we were accommodated with such late notice❤️❤️

@yan_stewi_travels
Fantastic peaceful spot, a bit dated, but the view was amazing.
Staff are very friendly and helpful. Picturesque location. Facilities showing their age but kept very clean.
Fairly close to the tourist attractions of Albany. Overlooking Albany city. Excellent views and clean area, toilets. Perfect parks.

On comprend bien pourquoi leur nom est "Panorama"... la vue y est magnifique. Emplacement trés bien situé qui malgré la popularité du mieux ne gâche aucunement la vue. Espaces communs spacieux et propres. La jetty était en travaux mais ça n'au aucunement gâché le séjour... la plage y est belle et l'eau claire. Les pélicans ajoutent un charme a l'endroit. Merveilleux
Dog friendly

Character-filled older style campground with permanent residents in a variety of cute cottages surrounded by flower gardens and vegie patches. Waterfront van/tent sites are unpowered but huge, with unbeatable views across the placid bay. Decent clean amenities including en-suite shower/toilets. Super helpful friendly staff. A real homely feel to this place, and while it’s a 15 minute drive to Albany it’s perfectly positioned for all the natural Torndirrup NP coast attractions, hikes and the whaling station museum.

We have enjoyed our stay at this park. Yes, it's old. But we found the reception friendly and helpful, the facilities clean and the permanent residents quiet and responsible. We only stayed two nights but wished we had booked longer. We had a lovely unpowered waterfront site which was HUGE! We also liked the location away from town but handy to the National Park attractions.

Die küche ist eine Katastrophe. Dreckig und alt… nichts daran ist schön. Sehr wenig bäder für viele leute. Unser stellplatz war krumm und schief, da konnte man nicht gut stehen. Ausserdem sehr viele dauerhafte bewohner, was eine komische Stimmung auf dem platz macht. Einzig schönes ist die nähe am wasser…

After setting up we witnessed one of the residents whip and beat his dog senseless whilst seemingly on drugs.

We just left and stayed elsewhere.

The lady on reception was new and lovely. But the place itself is awful.

Good park with all the usual facilities.
Manta Rays come right up to the door by the jetty.
Nice caravan park, close to the beach, good views and plenty of room

Booked for 3 nights, only stayed for 2. After making a booking several weeks in advance, we were allocated the worst site in the park! Hemmed in by enormous vans and dilapidated permanent sites, with nowhere to sit and no view, opposite the office and toilets, we asked to move to empty sites twice and were refused....no discussion. Don't be fooled by the pictures on their website....while there are great spots, there's no guarantee you'll get one! Very unaccomodating hosts!
Older park . Excellent location for Caravan . Very quiet park. Older facilities but very well maintained. Got walked to our site. Power and water hookup.
Fantastic quiet spot on the water.

Panorama Caravan Park exceeded all our expectations! We stayed for 3 nights and had a fantastic time. The waterfront view from our generously sized site was incredible. What truly set this park apart was the exceptional service. The hosts provided everything we needed, including electricity and water hookups, along with the hoses and cables to connect our camper trailer. They even supplied a movable grey water tank, a thoughtful touch we haven't encountered elsewhere. To have the hoses and electrical cables provided is completely outside of my experience of other caravan parks. The location is perfect for exploring the Albany region, and the peaceful atmosphere made our stay truly enjoyable. We'll definitely be back!

My husband, myself and our fur baby had the pleasure of staying in this hidden gem for 3 nights.
It was to wind down after a big holiday before returning home.
Jasmine welcomed us and walked us to our sight.
Jasmine was absolutely lovely and answered all my questions.
Our sight was situated on a concrete slab in front of permanent residents.
The residents were extremely friendly and welcoming.
The view was just so relaxing and the pelicans did not disappoint. Looking out over Frenchman’s bay was so peaceful and at night when the lights went on was lovely.
The bathroom facilities were dated but when I used them were clean.
The park was situated out of town but again for us in a perfect location.
We would like to thankyou for having us and will definitely return.
Jasmine we took your advice on Mount Barkers Bakery on our way home and omg amazing 😋
Nice water view. Caravan park is mostly residential and run down, only a small portion is tourists. Bathrooms are pretty standard and camp kitchen is basic. Good for back packers. Very overpriced!!
Relaxing, waterfront views. Was a perfect spot to base nearby to the national park. Clean/ easy to access amenities and friendly staff.

Great camp site right on the ocean.
The staff were really friendly and facilities good!
the best view from the powered van sites!!
Fantastic location with stunning views across Frenchman Bay of Albany. A little dated but clean and tidy. Nice grassy sites for tents or caravans.
Amazing caravanpark in Frenchman Bay, beautifully maintained grounds, immaculatly clean facilities..friendly staff, and dog friendly 😀

If I could give 6 out of 5 stars I would. Albany’s little secret when it comes to a place to stay with stunning views from the huge campsites and all at a very very good price compared to other caravan parks around the area. Yes it has basic amenities but they are spotlessly clean and there are showers that are actually hot with great water pressure. The staff are super friendly and happy to do what they can to make your stay relaxing and enjoyable. Stay for 1 night or stay for a week, you’ll love your stay and have it earmarked as your place to stay each time you’re in town.
Old school caravan Park but beautiful with large sites and super friendly service.

We had an excellent camping experience at Panorama. Our level site was excellent with a great view. The manager was super helpful and provided lots of ideas on places to visit. The camp kitchen was clean and well maintained.

Lots of space for a caravan and the staff were all so nice!!! Close to Albany for day trips. Quiet spot. Large camp kitchen and ensuite were good for families. Jetty is currently closed. Lots of pelicans. Bring a water catcher for your caravan. Bit too shallow for fishing.  Thank you so much!

We had some of the best service we have ever had at a caravan park from Anita!
We also had a grass waterfront site with so much space. Best caravan park!

Paid $35 for a patch of grass,hot shower.  No powered sites available,at the time no pension discounts or for multiple nights, she also failed to tell me they had a camp kitchen which could of been very helpful. I felt it was overpriced.
Stunning location.i was waiting for my friend to come home from work to look at my options to stay another night,Deserie the front lady rang asking if I could vacate as there fully booked up for tonight,she was very abrupt and rude.I was left in a very awkward position as had nowhere else to go.
Came back to have dinner with friend and the patch of grass I was allocated and all surrounding grass was vacant. What a liar.t I was a quiet and respectfull Tennant. I won't return or recommend this place,I also took photos of the empty large grassed area that apparently was fully booked,just confirms she lied.
I hope Duncan reads this being the owner.

I would love to give a 5 star as location is amazing and this one of the very few places that provides a common space in a barn with sitting, books and even equipped kids corner. On the other hand I agree with others that kitchen, ablution and surroundings could be better maintained.

Annita was the loveliest person ever! She was so happy to help and went above and beyond to help us! The campsite was lovely and the view was amazing even in bad weather! Shower and bathroom facilities were good and they had good kitchen facilities! Loved 😝
I love the setup by the lake; it’s relaxing to watch the pelicans at the end of the day. The bathroom is well-maintained, and the owner is pretty helpful and welcoming. I would definitely return!
The spot we were allocated was perfect. By the bank and flat ground we thoroughly enjoyed our stay there.

Panorama was located exactly where we preferred - away from the busy-ness of lovely Albany, past a particularly lovely whiskey and gin distillery, near the Wind Farm coastal walks and the beautiful National Park access….all with a stunning view of both the National park coastline and Albany itself (amazing at night too). We were welcomed with helpful insight into local eateries and things to do and see, with obvious passion for the nature elements and tourist experience by friendly and accessible hosts. Didn’t want to leave our pelicans at the end of our pier!

Wonderful staff, have heaps of info on best places to visit.. National park walks right down to getting the best coffee in town. Managed my extended stay without a problem.. Close to fabulous Bald head walk, see pics
Stunning location, fantastic service by park management

Vans and cabins are crammed in with few trees making for an unappealing setting. Camping spaces are not defined, so its a bit of a dogs breakfast. The Park is close to Torndirrup NP and historic whale station. Adequate showers and toilet facilities. Camp kitchen is pretty rough.

Highly recommended to stay with your campervan or RVs. The staff were so helpful with booking and made our life easy. The facilities are clean and plenty of rooms are available in the shed to watch TV and have meals. You also get an amazing view as the site is facing the ocean.
Fantastic site with beautiful views and a fantastic host to top it all off.  The sites are very spacious and I highly recommend to put on your to visit list.  Deserves a 5 Star.

Damage was done to our $800 Stone Stomper by a whipper snipper operator. This was reported on the morning of our departure, it was photographed by a bloke called Duncan. He later told me that our site had not been touched the day this happened , however there was grass all over the top of it. I am not a liar, no compensation was offered, VERY DISSAPOINTED,
BE CAREFUL IF YOU GO THERE AND HAVE ANYTHING ON THE GROUND NEAR GRASS !
Bloody awesome spot to explore Albany
Perfect . Relaxed.
Cheap and Cheerful
A beautiful spot . Awesome management that are very accommodating thank you 🤩

I had the best most relaxing experience
Me and my partner stayed for 2 nights and it was the best 2 nights ever, fantastic service, beautiful camp-site right by the water
We were greeted by a lovely lady who showed us around and we were also greeted by a very cute dashhound named Henry he was very nice and welcoming, just like his owner
100% recommend this beautiful and relaxing camp-site you won't be disappointed at all thankyou so so much for such an amazing time we will definitely be back ❤️😊
The amenities were good, just not enough of them. The staff were great, and great location.

Panorama Caravan Park boasts some of the most stunning and breathtaking views of the bay right from your campsite. As the sun sets, the beautiful Albany nightscape comes to life. The friendly, warm, and accommodating hosts make you feel right at home. The ensuite-style bathroom facilities are a luxurious touch, with hot and strong showers. This park is definitely worth a visit for those seeking a scenic and comfortable camping experience.
Quite amazed that I was able to secure this location. The host was amazing, showed us around the area and we felt safe. The sunrise at the pier was 7/10.
Bonza caravan Park. Great location right on the water. Facilities are old but very clean. Good people run this park 👏
Nice position right on the sth side ot princess royal harbour with views of Albany grassed and tidy sites

This location was so perfect for us that we extended our stay from 3 nights to 6 nights. Our sites overlooking the water were magical. Desi was just a dream, giving us so much information about what to do around Albany and the special places to visit. She was an absolute goldmine of information. The amenities may not be the greatest in the universe, but my goodness, the soul is everything in this wonderful caravan park.

Love the scenery & how peaceful this place is while overlooking to the city from afar. Nothing fancy, just simple life and a nice escape from town.
Lovely people around. Desiree is the best! Thank you for your support
Beautiful spot 🤩
Annita was incredible to deal with,very willing to help. An awesome family run caravan park. Highly recommend

Booked in for 2 nights in a perfect site over looking Albany’s lights at night and beautiful sunrise. Greeted by Des very friendly and personally took us to our site, what information she gave us about Albany things to see and do very informative very helpful. Great location for a caravan park we are definitely coming back.

Very nice place to stay in Albany. We stayed on powered site and can’t complain :) hot and clean showers with lot of light, superkind lady in the office.
And also - the best price you can find in Albany.
Was a lovely stay! We just stayed here for one night in the roof top tent. Beautiful view over the inlet, very clean bathrooms and kitchen facilities, hot showers and friendly staff

I can't recommend Panorama highly enough, would give more than five stars if we could. Beautiful setting on the water, Birdlife, quiet at night and so friendly. Staff are helpful, friendly and generous. Atmosphere is wonderful. If you stay here, you won't be sorry..a gem.

This is a great little spot 2 minutes outside Torrindup National Park. Staff are lovely and accommodating. Sites are right down by the water for campers and tents, great sunrises!! Would definitely stay again.
We will be back. What a great place to unwind from driving all day. The inlet was calm and colourful.

Camp site is clean, well maintained and has stunning views. Staff are friendly and very accommodating. Only complaint is a resident caravan having their tv blairing late into the night making sleep difficult.

This campground is so pretty!!! You literally park next to the water. It’s peaceful, economical and full of pelicans!
The host was so nice and helpful, she gave me the impression she really cares and loves her job!
I wish i stayed longer !! Great experience that i truly recommend!

This is my second time staying at Panorama Caravan Park and it was fantastic! We were camping in a tent right on the waterfront with amazing views. With the right weather - not to much wind - it makes for an amazing site.
The staff are so helpful and friendly offering to tell you about the sites of Albany and give you a detailed tour of the park.
The amenities are dated, but they are clean, have great hot water and pressure.
The sites are well spaced with plenty of room and I will be staying there again next time I'm in Albany.
Very nice, great amenities, awesome for the kids, awesome location and reasonable price.

Belle place pour rester pour quelques nuits proche d’Albany. Le personnel est très sympa et accueillant !
Beaucoup de vent dans la journée mais plutôt calme en soirée, nous avons pu nous abriter sous le shed pour manger et passer la soirée.
Toilettes et douches propres (mais pas nombreux…).
Super lever du soleil au matin !

Great little caravan park, hosts were so friendly and helpful. Lots of permanent vans, but the bookable sites are in a different section, close to the water (which gives a great view but was very windy on some days). There are a few sites which are more suited to caravans so might be best to  call to see which site you can get, as others are not so flat and there are also trees which can be a challenge for positioning a caravan. However, this means most sites are perfect for swags, swags, tents, vans or roof top tents, and the park has a great atmosphere for this type of set up. Amenities are older style but clean and well looked after. Close to the National parks too.

Desiree was such a cheerful person. We felt right at home.
The kitchen could use an update
Very friendly and helpful hosts, amazing views across Frenchman's bay to Albany. Toilets were clean, hot water, good pressure. Sites are near and tidy. Excellent caravan park!
Great views, friendly staff, peaceful spot with nice spacing between camps, close to whale museum, National parks & Goode beach is well worth a swim or just a visit, not to far from town

Positive experience! Friendly service at reception.
We checked in late but the receptionist was still in the office and was happy to sort us out.
Our powered camp site by the water was huge.
Amenities were clean and tidy.

If you seek clean hot showers and toilets, this is your place, they are not fancy, they are cheap and their service is outstanding.

We had a great week in this park, the weather was perfect.

The reception on arrival was brilliant and very helpful.  Daily the toilets and showers are cleaned and while not modern, they are clean and work.

Thank you for a great week.
Couldn’t fault my stay here. Stunning location, very clean, spacious, green grass, beautiful sunrises, lovely staff and super chilled.

We had a great 2 night stay at this campsite. The views from our site were just stunning. The facilities are not fancy but the toilet facilities were clean and in good working order. Staff very friendly and helpful!

From the initial enquiry - until the time we left, the staff were amazingly friendly and helpful!!
Thanks ladies,  we'll be back some time soon 👌.
Perfect location to explore the local attractions,  great view of the city lights across the harbour,  peaceful and friendly.

Great location and Duncan was friendly, and very detailed instructions were given to access our campsite. However, kitchen was very dirty, and no cooking utensils were provided. Camping location was really windy too, making it hard to set up the tents

Two of the best hosts that we have come across the owners could not do enough for you and the site and view where to die for.

Regards
Brett

Unfortunately I need to write a bad review, even though the staff was super nice to us and the view from our campsite (right in front of the water) was amazing. There is only one toilet and one shower per gender and three small unisex toilets/showers. None of them offers soap and hand dryer/paper towels. Also the campkitchen was the dirtiest we saw on our 4 months of travelling through Oz. It was simply disgusting. The price is way too high for what you get, especially when it would be so easy to adjust some of the main things (offer soap & clean the campkitchen regularly). I hope you take my bad review as inspiration to improve. We would love to come back one das as the view was amazing.

Duncan, Annita and crew are some of the most welcoming and friendly people we have ever come across. Nothing was too much trouble and we were treated like old friends. The view was something special, and you couldn’t be closer to some of the best Albany has to offer. Although Mother Nature threw a bit of wind at us, and we had to move to a more sheltered part of the park, we had an excellent stay. Facilities were always clean and in good working order not fancy but if you want fancy book a hotel!

We will certainly be back!
Weather was against us. I’m sure it’s a great spot in anything other than a brisk easterly. Had to reduce our intended stay but will be back to try again.

파워 캠핑장을 처음 와봤는데 👍
샤워장도 화장실도 전기도 물도 취사 시설고 넓고 다 깨끗하고 좋습니다. 호스트께서 반갑게 맞아주시고 주변 관광지들을 오전 오후별로 추천과 루트도 짜주셨습니다. Rpg 게임 첫 입문자를 위한 친절한 NPC 같습니다. 아주 친절하고 나이스한 사람입니다. 추천 장소들 루트대로 시간대별로 다녀왔는데 아주 만족스러웠습니다. 강력 추천 드립니다!
Stunning location, the highlight was the incredible people running the site. Full of great recommendations and so happy to help.

Stayed here for 3 nig hts  and were very satisfied with our stay.  Our site was overlooking the bay with plenty of grass.  Amenities are old but very clean with ample hot water and very practical showers but do lack soap.  Duncan, the manager was super enthusiastic and could not do enough for us.  Good rates and I would highly recommend this park.

Absolutely fantastic spot Duncan the host is fantastic great old fashioned customer service  at its best. Very friendly loves a chat and he walks you to your camp spot. Very knowledgeable about the area and can recommend some great things to do in the area.
Great spot. Can't speak highly Enough of Duncan I left my glasses in the bathroom and he arranged to post them to my home in Perth.
Not worth to go there . Not a level ground surface . Kitchen and toilets are very dirty . I kept my food in fridge next day it was stolen . Don't have a CCTV camera for security Not really recommend

Not a clean site the owner is very rude we booked for new year someone else offered him higher price than us
That’s why he asked us to leave the park
Happy friendly staff and a beautiful quiet location
I really in love the  campsite view. Very beautiful and peaceful in  the morning. Everything is so clean, restrooms, kitchen and also they're friendly and helpful.Thank you 💕🍀
Very basic ablutions but the manager, Duncan, and the place more than offset that. Very helpful and clean, would definitely stay again.

This was a really picturesque park filled with permanent residents and tourists like us.
Duncan the owner (probably the most ocker bloke I've met) was extremely passionate about the place and gave us great advice for the area.
Close to a lot of walks and natural beauty.
Thoroughly recommended.

Super serene location. So peaceful on the lake, with a view of Albany. Beautiful spot to have a quiet weekend (or two) away from it all. Super friendly and approachable manager with lots of great local info having been here since before Y2K. Fire pits (BYO raised one or there are a few around the grounds) are usable, which adds to the peaceful night time atmosphere.

This is one of the best caravan parks we have stayed at.  Not because of the facilities but because of the location and the friendliness of the owners and staff.  Allowing caravans to set up with the best views of the bay was wonderful. The pelicans are amazing. The location and accessibility to the Nat Park was fantastic.  So thank you Panoraama for a memorable stay.  Also he lights across the water to Albany at night was a gift too.  Cheers fromCasey and Jill [Victorians enjoying WA)

I am giving this park 5 stars as Duncan was so accommodating. From the moment we arrived we were made to feel at home. Location by the water was perfect. Camp kitchen needs an update but everything else makes up for it.

Host is sooo funky n nice, show us everything and arrange us camp right in front of the water, we had a such beautiful time here, really really enjoyed it, amazing location for explore national park, recommended!
We had a few days here recently.  The owner was lovely and very helpful. We had a site right down by the water. So peaceful. Amenities were very  👍  thanks guys. Keep up the good work.
Loved this park friendly staff and residents stunning view thank you

We got the best spot for our camper by the water! The sunrise was to die for. Three large pelicans accompanied us at breakfast. The camp kitchen is rudimentary - a big tin shed which does not keep out wind and cold, and a few dusty frying pans. However it has lovely hot water taps for washing up in the dining shed, which is nice. We used the showers in the same building as the reception. These are renovated and simply delightful! They have a heating! Cheaper in price than other holiday parks in Albany. It's about 15 min drive to Albany town. Perfect base to explore the whaling station, Gap, and blowholes. Owner is exceedingly enthusiastic (almost supernaturally so) and very responsive to calls. No security boomgate or passcodes. Overall a wonderful campsite because of the view.

Stayed one night on unpowered site, enjoyed the sea view, the place surrounded by quiet, peace and calm, beautiful nature and close to Torndirrup National Park, which mean you have an easy access to many tourist spots eg. The Gap and Natural Bridge, Cave Point Lighthouse, Blowholes, Whaling Station, Misery Beach, Frenchman Bay, Salmon Holes, Wind Farm (the list goes on lol 😆).
Staff was friendly, even it was last minute booking, it almost dark and heavy rain but she welcomed us with smile.
Place equipped with basic amenities but glad they have good working hot water and shower, also complete with basic kitchen stuffs, microwave, kettle, cooking utensils etc. They keep the place clean and well-maintained. Thank you to owners and staff. Keep up the good job! Hopefully will have a chance to come again one day and stay longer next time. Definitely recommended 👍👍👍
